behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module handoff: three source adapters/feeders must all populate the aggregation payload before summary generation. In-process stubs for each source are sufficient to expose missing/overwritten fields. Rejected System because behavior text is about payload composition, not real network/OS effects; rejected Agentic because this check is pre-summary input completeness, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collector + aggregator modules with stubbed clients for metrics API, issue tracker API, and deployment log reader. Return distinct sentinel values from each source; assert the exact object/string sent into summary-generation contains all three sentinel sections with correct mapping.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/error-propagation across modules (source fetch -> assembler -> renderer), so Unit is too low. A controlled in-process failure (exception/timeout from one source client) can reproduce the real decision path and verify graceful degradation. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network stack behavior, only unavailable-source handling semantics; rejected Agentic because pass/fail is fallback note presence and pipeline continuation, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline with two sources returning normal data and one source client stub raising an unavailable error. Assert process does not abort, output report is generated, and the affected section contains `data unavailable` while other sections keep real values.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on summary content quality (whether generated text preserves key values), which is a model decision boundary. Stubbed deterministic responses would hide hallucination/omission errors. Rejected Workflow because behavior describes one summary-generation decision, not multiple agentic decisions chained.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixed hard-case canary set of input payloads with tricky numeric/value contrasts (e.g., close percentages, opposing trend directions, missing fields). Call the real summary model and score outputs against value-preservation checks (key numbers/entities/trends must match source payload). Fail on omissions, swaps, or fabricated values.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary includes external HTTP/service calls and real email delivery/inbox receipt, which mocks can falsely pass. This is end-to-end infrastructure validation. Rejected Agentic as minimum because inbox delivery correctness can be validated without depending on model-quality assertions; rejected Workflow because behavior does not require validating multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled job in a production-like environment against reachable test endpoints and a real test mailbox. Assert end-to-end completion, message receipt in recipient inbox, and expected report sections present in delivered email body/attachment.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real filesystem persistence plus cross-run retry behavior tied to scheduler/execution environment; pure mocks can miss path/permission/atomic-write/restart issues. Rejected Integration because wording explicitly requires next scheduled run semantics and durable local fallback; rejected Workflow because retry logic is temporal orchestration, not multiple agentic model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run 1: make email service unreachable (connection failure) and execute job; assert report is written to configured local fallback path. Run 2 (next schedule trigger): restore email service and execute again; assert pending saved report is retried and successfully delivered, then fallback state is cleared/marked sent.
